zh.lifeSummary: Isabel Lady Willoughby是Sir Richard Willoughby的女兒，於1315年出生在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾。她於1340年與Sir Reginald de Monboucher在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾維洛賀比 曼諾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1419年在英國達勒姆比阿米申享年104歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Isabel Lady Willoughby是Sir Richard Willoughby的女儿，于1315年出生在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾。她于1340年与Sir Reginald de Monboucher在英国英格兰林可爾史爾維洛賀比 曼諾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1419年在英国達勒姆比阿米申享年104岁。

nameMeanings.name: Willoughby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Willoughby, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, Nottinghamshire, and Warwickshire. They are named from an Old English +wilig ‘willow’ + Old Norse bȳ ‘farmstead, village’.

variants.name: Wilby
meanings: habitational name from any of the places called Wilby, in Suffolk, Norfolk, and Northamptonshire. The first placename is probably named from an Old English wilig ‘willow’ + Old English bēag ‘circle, ring’; the Norfolk placename derives from Old English wilig + bēag or Old Norse bý ‘farmstead, village’; the Northamptonshire placename derives from the Old Norse personal name Vili + bý.
Redmonds has demonstrated that in the parish of Hipperholme, and subsequently elsewhere in Yorkshire, Wilby occurs as a variant of Willoughby .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Isabel Lady Willoughby was born in 1315, in Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Sir Richard Willoughby. She married Sir Reginald de Monboucher in 1340, in Willoughby Manor, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1419, in Beamish, Durham, England, at the age of 104.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Lady Willoughbyは1315年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアでSir Richard Willoughbyの娘として生まれた。1340年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアウィロウビー マノーでSir Reginald de Monboucherと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1419年にイギリスダルハムビーミッシュで104歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Lady Willoughby(은)는 1315년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르에서 Sir Richard Willoughby의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 윌루그h비 먼어에서 Sir Reginald de Monboucher(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1419년 영국 뒤람 브이미시에서 104세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabel Lady Willoughby родилась в 1315 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. Её отца звали Sir Richard Willoughby. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Reginald de Monboucher в 1340 г. в местности уиллоугби манор, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1419 г. в местности бимиш, дурам, Англия в возрасте 104 лет.
